title: Speedup Factors in Practice
concept: Speculative decoding typically achieves a 1.5x to 2.5x speedup in production, depending on draft model accuracy. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Model Distillation.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using speculative decoding?

options.text: To speed up token generation using two models.
To make the main model use less computer memory.

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding uses a small, fast model to suggest tokens and a large, slow model to verify them, aiming to speed up the process.

cards.title: How Fast is Fast?
cards.body: We know speculative decoding speeds up AI by using a smaller draft model to guess words. But how much faster does it actually run when deployed in real applications? Let's look at the numbers!

cards.title: Speedup Factors in Practice
cards.body: In real-world applications, speculative decoding does not double or triple speed automatically. In practice, it usually speeds up generation by 1.5x to 2.5x. This speedup is highly dependent on how accurately the draft model can guess the main model's words.

cards.title: The Dictation Assistant
cards.body: Imagine you are a slow, master writer dictating a book, and you hire a speedy assistant who tries to guess your next words. If the assistant is really smart (high accuracy), you only have to correct them occasionally, and you finish the book twice as fast. If the assistant makes bad guesses, you waste time crossing out their work, and your speedup drops.

cards.title: Real-World Chatbot Response
cards.body: Consider a customer service chatbot. Without speculative decoding, it answers a query in 4 seconds. With a well-tuned draft model achieving a 70% acceptance rate, it answers in just 2 seconds—a solid 2x speedup that makes the chatbot feel much more conversational.

cards.title: The 10x Speedup Myth
cards.body: Many people assume that using a draft model will always guarantee a massive 5x or 10x speed boost. In reality, draft models make mistakes. Correcting these mistakes creates overhead, which caps typical real-world speedups at around 1.5x to 2.5x.

cards.title: Accuracy vs. Speedup
cards.body: Here is how draft model accuracy impacts the final speedup in a production pipeline.

left.text: Fewer rejections, fewer rollbacks. The main model quickly approves guesses, yielding a 2.2x speedup.

right.text: Frequent guess mismatches. The main model spends time correcting mistakes, dropping speedup down to 1.1x.

exercises.prompt: What is the typical speedup range observed when using speculative decoding in production?
exercises.explain: Speculative decoding generally delivers a 1.5x to 2.5x speedup in production, depending on how often the draft model guesses correctly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A lower accuracy draft model will result in a lower speedup factor.
exercises.explain: If a draft model makes poor guesses, the main model has to reject them and regenerate the text, which reduces the overall speedup.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about speculative decoding performance.
exercises.sentence: The real-world speedup of speculative decoding depends heavily on the draft model's accuracy.
exercises.explain: The speedup factor is directly tied to the accuracy of the draft model. If it guesses correctly, the main model accepts its work, and generation goes faster.

exercises.prompt: Identify the realistic performance report for a production-ready speculative decoding setup.

options.text: Draft Model A: Achieves a 1.0x speedup with a 10% acceptance rate.
Draft Model B: Achieves a 1.8x speedup with a 75% acceptance rate.
Draft Model C: Achieves a 15.0x speedup with a 99% acceptance rate.

exercises.explain: Draft model B is behaving like a typical speculative decoding setup, achieving a 1.8x speedup. Option A shows no speedup, and Option C shows an unrealistically high 15x speedup.

exercises.prompt: Based on production data, what is the typical speedup range achieved by speculative decoding in practice?
exercises.explain: In real-world systems, speculative decoding brings a practical speedup of 1.5x to 2.5x. High draft accuracy means fewer rollbacks, saving time! Next up: Model Distillation.
